_comment: Declarative exec-approval policy. The agent classifies a high-risk action (by --kind, or by pattern-matching a --command string) and this file decides allow|confirm|deny PER ACCESS TIER. 'confirm' = pause and request async owner sign-off in #approvals before proceeding. Edit freely; check_policy.py reads it on every call (no restart). SECURITY POSTURE: the default is FAIL-CLOSED ('confirm') — an action the rules don't recognize is held for owner sign-off, never silently allowed. Add explicit allow-rules for known-safe action classes if the confirm volume is too high; do NOT flip the default back to 'allow'.

rules.summary: Send or schedule an outbound email
Post to an external/social surface or message a non-owner (X/Twitter, SMS, outbound DM)
Delete or overwrite/truncate files, or destructively reset a repo
Initiate a payment, purchase, or financial transaction
Push or open a PR against an upstream third-party repo (e.g. sonichi/*), or merge any PR
Read a secret/token/key into output or send it off-host
